howItWorks.intro: This anti-dumping duty calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Dumping Margin
rules.formula: Margin = (Fair Value - Export Price) / Export Price × 100
rules.explanation: Percentage by which export price is below fair market value.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is dumping?
faq.answer: Selling goods in a foreign market below fair value or below home market price.

faq.question: How are anti-dumping duties calculated?
faq.answer: The duty equals the difference between fair value and the export price.

faq.question: Is the Anti-Dumping Duty Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What export price should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate export price value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Anti-Dumping Duty Calculator?
sections.body: The Anti-Dumping Duty Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your export price, fair market value, quantity, you get instant results including dumping margin, duty per unit, total duty. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting dumping margin right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your export price and need to find the right dumping margin. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Export Price ($)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your export price in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Fair Market Value ($)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your fair market value in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Quantity (units)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your quantity in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your dumping margin, duty per unit, total duty. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your export price before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both dumping margin and duty per unit — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your quantity, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
